displayMetadata.description: Entities extracted from sample contracts
A generated string describing the alert cause and the metric change.
The description formatted for display in a Workshop markdown widget with object reference syntax.
The primary metric in which a deviation passed the threshold to create an alert.
The metric value floor threshold for this alert to trigger.
A string specifically created to follow the metric value and place it in context. Primarily used in Workshop metric card widget configuration as the "secondary" metric value.
The deviation metric normalized between 0 and 1 relative to all other deviation metrics that pass the floor threshold.
The deviation metric weighted by the route flight volume. Used to determine the relative priority and severity of the alert.
The deviation expressed as a percent change relative to the comparison period.
The percent change pre-formatted for display
The percent change threshold for this alert type to trigger.
Alerts generated based on historical route performance, structured as an operational object type to demonstrate basic status and assignment patterns.
Carriers with derived fleet metrics from associated aircraft. Full 2023 flight history available for a subset of carriers where the "Complete Flight History" property is "true".
This object provides information on learnings from historical aviation incidents.

If provided, this property will be populated with keywords from the rule logic, such as column names and filter values.This allows users to search for rules based on their logic, and so is highly recommended to provide this property

displayMetadata.description: Name your field
Describe purpose for this field. This will not be input to the LLM.

Free text types are used to extract a value in the document. ie date: 01/01/20XX, or claim id: 100XXX. Categorical types are used for discrete values to extract or infer. ie color: blue, green, red. You can define these categories by creating an Option.
Give a detailed description of the field. This text will be used to provide context when the extraction occurs.

Input the parent definition if there is a relationship between these entities. For example, a shipment destination definition can have a parent definition shipment id, making it easy to link the extracted values.
Assign this field to a process to be run alongside other field extractions.
